content: CALCULATION MANIFEST — SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH
--- All locked input and output values for this estimate. Sections 1 and 7 copy from here.
Assembly Complexity | Mixed bolt and weld
--- Project cost summary at fixed rate band.
Project Name or ID | HOME 2 - CAROWINDS
Assembly Complexity | Mixed bolt and weld
Fixed Rate Band Applied | $3,500 — $4,500 per ton
Cost per Fabricated Ton — Mid | $4,400 per ton

Top Three Commercial Risks: 1.  CRITICAL: All steel lintels (W-shapes and angles) are entirely unquantified. Schedules on S0.02 refer to Architectural drawings which were not provided. This uncosted scope could add 5-10 tons. 2.  CRITICAL: Column lengths are not specified in the Steel Column Schedule (S0.02). A full building height of 43'-0" was assumed for all primary columns based on elevations. Incorrect lengths will significantly alter tonnage. 3.  WARNING: Several HSS profiles (HSS18x6x3/8, HSS9x5x3/8, HSS6x6x5/16, HSS6x6x5/8, HSS8x6x3/8) are not in the provided AISC unit weight table. Weights were sourced externally and require verification.

Tonnage extraction method: (c) Estimated from framing plans where BOM is absent or incomplete. All quantities and lengths for beams and columns were counted and scaled directly from framing plans S1.01 and S1.02. Schedules on S0.02 were used for member profiles and plate sizes but were critically incomplete, lacking quantities and lengths.

Material (Carbon Steel): Explicitly stated in General Notes on S0.01 (ASTM A992, A500, A36). Surface Treatment (Shop primer only): Stated as the primary finish in General Note 11 on S0.01. The requirement for galvanized bearing plates and zinc-rich primer on some beam ends is noted as a risk but assumed to be covered within the rate band. Assembly Complexity (Mixed bolt and weld): Assumed from various details. S0.02 shows a bolted Wind Post connection, while the Lintel Schedule implies welded bottom plates. Canopies and trellises (S3.05, S3.06) show a mix of shop-welded and field-bolted assemblies.

Rate basis: Fixed rate band of $3,500 (low) to $4,500 (high) per fabricated ton applied. Midpoint rate of $4,000 per fabricated ton used for the headline cost. These rates represent current USA structural steel shop fabrication pricing for the member types and complexity class identified in this package.

All column lengths are assumed based on building elevation data, as they are absent from schedules. Weights for HSS sections not in the provided reference table have been sourced from external AISC data. HSS9x5x3/8 on S1.02 is assumed to be a typo for a standard size; weight for HSS10x4x3/8 was used. Cap plates (1/2" thick) are assumed for all HSS columns as none were specified.

--- Key Assumptions (minimum five specific items — cite sheet references):

Column lengths are not defined on schedule S0.02. A full building height of 43'-0" (13,106 mm) was assumed for all columns on grids B and C and wind posts, derived from roof elevation on S2.03. Rate band of $3,500 to $4,500 per fabricated ton represents current USA market pricing for the member types, surface treatment, and assembly complexity identified in this estimate. Quantities for all members were derived by counting elements on plans S1.01 and S1.02, as schedules on S0.02 lack quantities. HSS9x5x3/8 shown on S1.02 is assumed to be a non-standard designation; the weight for HSS10x4x3/8 was used for costing. This requires clarification. All HSS columns are assumed to receive a 1/2" thick cap plate for beam connections, as none are shown or specified on S0.02.
Exclusions — Items NOT included in this estimate:

Site erection labour, cranes, manlifts, rigging, and flagging. Professional engineer stamping or delegated connection design. Loose anchor bolts cast-in-place by the general contractor. Site touch-up painting after final erection. Tax or duties beyond 0% applied in this estimate. 3D MEP coordination and cross-trade clash detection. Inflation beyond 30 calendar days from the date of this estimate. CRITICAL EXCLUSION: All steel lintels (W8x24, W16x50, L7x4, L6x4, L4x4) specified on S0.02 schedules are EXCLUDED from this estimate due to missing quantities and lengths, which are deferred to un-provided architectural drawings.

Tonnage increases or decreases exceeding 5% from this estimate. Coating system upgrade — for example, from primer-only to hot-dip galvanised. Clarification of lintel schedule quantities and lengths from architectural drawings. Addition of member categories not shown on the current drawing set. Revision of drawings that changes profile sizes, lengths, or quantities.

--- This estimate is preliminary and carries high commercial risk due to significant gaps in the provided structural drawings. The tonnage and cost are based on multiple necessary assumptions, particularly regarding column lengths and the complete exclusion of the steel lintel package. Before this estimate is used for a binding bid, the owner must provide complete schedules or architectural drawings. The top priority is to issue RFIs to clarify all missing quantities, lengths, and non-standard profiles. If the lintel package is added or column lengths change, this estimate must be re-run with the updated inputs.
